plan.reasoning: No prior attempts exist, so I must propose the best physically sensible initial plan. The user wants the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for NNH on Cu3Ag(111). The provided SMILES is [N]=[NH], which corresponds to diazenyl/imide-like NNH, not a closed-shell saturated species. SMILES consistency check: atom 0 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 1; atom 1 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 0. There is no mismatch with the requested NNH description. Because atom 0 has an unpaired electron, ReactiveSpecies treatment is appropriate if binding through atom 0. Atom 1 does not have an unpaired electron, so it should not be selected as the binding atom for ReactiveSpecies. For NNH-like species on coinage/alloy metal surfaces, the terminal radical N typically binds end-on through the radical center rather than side-on through the N=N unit, especially as an initial low-energy guess. On Cu/Ag alloys, Cu is generally more reactive than Ag, so a Cu ontop site is the strongest first candidate among the listed available sites. Site naming must use canonical names only, so I choose ontop. This satisfies the site alignment rule with one adsorbate binding index. Therefore the best initial and only allowed attempt is end-on adsorption of atom 0 on a Cu ontop site.
